# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to this project.

We welcome different kinds of contributions, including:
- bug fixes
- new features
- documentation improvements
- knowledge base articles
- examples, guides, and best practices

## Finding Something to Contribute
Planned article ideas and content tasks are tracked in the project board:
- [Knowledge Hub Project Board](https://github.com/users/SFITDelivery/projects/1)

Contributors are welcome to choose topics from the `Backlog` column.

Please do not start work on items already marked as `In Progress` unless this has been discussed with a maintainer first.

For larger articles or substantial content updates, please leave a comment or open an issue before starting work to avoid duplication and confirm that the topic is still relevant and aligned with current priorities.

## Before You Start
For large changes, new features, significant structural updates, or architecture-related proposals, please open an issue first so the idea can be discussed before substantial work begins.

## Contribution Workflow
1. Fork the repository.
2. Create a new branch from `main`.
3. Make your changes.
4. Verify that your changes work as expected.
5. Open a pull request targeting `main`.

## Branch Naming
Please use clear and descriptive branch names, for example:
- `fix/short-description`
- `feat/short-description`
- `docs/short-description`

## Pull Request Guidelines
Please keep pull requests focused on a single topic.

When opening a pull request, include:
- a clear summary of what changed
- the reason for the change
- steps to validate or review the change
- a reference to the related issue, if one exists

## Documentation and Content Updates
If your contribution changes behavior, setup instructions, workflows, or user-facing functionality, please update the relevant documentation in the same pull request.

For knowledge base or documentation contributions:
- keep content clear and practical
- use a consistent structure and formatting style
- verify technical accuracy before submission

## Style and Consistency
Please follow the existing project structure, naming conventions, writing style, and formatting used in the repository.

Avoid mixing unrelated changes in a single pull request.

## Review Process
All contributions are reviewed before merging.

Maintainers may request clarifications, revisions, or additional changes during review before a pull request can be accepted.

## Code of Conduct
By participating in this project, you agree to communicate respectfully and constructively in issues, pull requests, and discussions.

## Questions
If you are unsure whether a contribution is a good fit, please open an issue first and describe your idea.
